title: umbilicus vulgaris subsp. pendulinus
intro: Umbilicus vulgaris subsp. pendulinus, commonly known as pendulous ivy-leaved crane's-bill, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Crassulaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, promoting wound healing, and easing symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. In herbal preparations, pendulous ivy-leaved crane's-bill is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
